title: Facing authority: a theory of political legitimacy [Book Review]
author: Hyde, Saracino

opening_move.description: The review opens by situating Fossen's book within non-ideal theory, framing political legitimacy as a pressing practical question in conditions of institutional fragility and democratic backsliding.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: In conditions of turbulence, we need practical accounts of how to face authority when justice and consensus are precarious

theoretical_positioning.description: The review presents Fossen's 'facing authority' approach appreciatively — legitimacy as a practical orientation toward authority rather than an abstract concept — without substantial independent critique.

confidence_notes: Book review genre; primarily expository. The contemporary political framing (pandemic, wars, backsliding) functions as rhetorical scene-setting.
